insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: high tide level is raising risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls; hours since high tide is lowering risk — the timing relative to high tide affects how concentrated bacteria are at the shoreline; and limited sunlight is raising risk (less UV to disinfect the water). The most recent sample, 4 days ago, was excellent at 5 MPN/100mL.
